name: aapt
title: aapt
description:
Android Asset Packaging Tool
The Android Asset Packaging Tool (aapt) takes your application resource
files, such as the AndroidManifest.xml file and the XML files for your
Activities, and compiles them. An R.java is also produced so you can
reference your resources from your Java code. This tool allows you to view,
create, and update Zip-compatible archives (zip, jar, apk). It can also
compile resources into binary assets.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use aapt because Android Asset Packaging Tool.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use aapt as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `aapt command [ options ] files ...` as documented upstream for aapt.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `aapt l[ist] [-v] [-a] file.{zip,jar,apk}` as documented upstream for aapt.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `aapt d[ump] [--values] WHAT file.{apk} [asset [asset ...]]` as documented upstream for aapt.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `aapt p[ackage] [-d] [-f] [-m] [-u] [-v] [-x] [-z] [-M AndroidManifest.xml]` as documented upstream for aapt.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair aapt with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `aapt --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install android-platform-frameworks-base. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `aapt command [ options ] files ...`. Further invocations from upstream: `aapt l[ist] [-v] [-a] file.{zip,jar,apk}`; `aapt d[ump] [--values] WHAT file.{apk} [asset [asset ...]]`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Android Asset Packaging Tool.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: Android Asset Packaging Tool.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install android-platform-frameworks-base
Install with: sudo apt install android-platform-frameworks-base
